name: Correlation Engine & Story Manager
description: Two tightly coupled modules plus a persistence layer: (1) correlation.py — matches incoming events to correlation rules by extracting group_by keys, O(1) lookup via dict keyed by (rule_name, key_tuple), creates new stories when no match found, implements chain_by (on story close, register chain_by keys so subsequent events link to new story with parent_story_id). (2) story_manager.py — manages open stories in bounded memory with max_open_stories and LRU eviction (OrderedDict), periodic async timeout sweep, closes stories on timeout/terminal event/eviction, emits closed stories to registered sink callbacks. Evicted stories emitted with status='evicted'. Event ordering by timestamp with arrival-time tiebreaker and monotonic sequence. (3) state.py — JSONL append-only persistence for story state records and replay-based recovery. Covers AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC8, AC14.

types.name: EventTime

fields.name: value
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime

types.description: UTC datetime string in ISO 8601 format representing when the event occurred at the source.

types.name: StoryId

fields.name: value
fields.description: UUID4 string identifier

validators.error_message: Must be a valid UUID4 string

types.description: Unique identifier for a story. UUID4 string by default, injectable factory for testing.

types.name: EventId

fields.name: value
fields.description: UUID4 string identifier

validators.error_message: Must be a valid UUID4 string

types.description: Unique identifier for an event. UUID4 string.

types.name: RuleName

fields.name: value
fields.description: Rule name identifier

validators.error_message: Rule name must start with lowercase letter, contain only lowercase alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen, max 128 chars

types.description: Name identifier for a correlation rule. Must be non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric with underscores/hyphens.

types.name: SequenceNumber

fields.name: value
fields.description: Non-negative monotonic counter

types.description: Monotonically increasing integer assigned by the engine to each event for deterministic ordering.

types.name: MaxOpenStories

fields.name: value
fields.description: Maximum number of open stories

types.description: Upper bound on simultaneously open stories in the StoryManager.

types.name: TimeoutSeconds

fields.name: value
fields.description: Positive duration in seconds

types.description: Timeout duration in seconds for story inactivity or chain TTL.

types.name: SweepIntervalSeconds

fields.name: value
fields.description: Positive interval in seconds

validators.error_message: Sweep interval must be between 0.1 and 3600 seconds

types.description: Interval in seconds between periodic timeout sweep passes.

types.name: MatchPredicate

fields.name: field
fields.description: Dot-delimited path into event payload (e.g. 'source_type' or 'payload.severity')

fields.name: value
fields.description: Exact value or regex pattern to match against

fields.name: is_regex
fields.description: If true, value is interpreted as a regex pattern

types.description: A single field-matching predicate within a correlation rule. Matches event payload field to an exact value or regex pattern.

types.name: TerminalCondition

fields.name: field
fields.description: Dot-delimited path into event payload

fields.name: value
fields.description: Exact value that triggers terminal closure

fields.name: is_regex
fields.description: If true, value is a regex pattern

types.description: Condition that, when matched by an event, triggers story closure with status 'completed'.

types.name: CorrelationRule

fields.name: name
fields.description: Unique rule identifier

fields.name: match
fields.description: List of MatchPredicate — all must match for the rule to apply to an event

fields.name: group_by
fields.description: List of event payload field paths used to construct the composite key for story lookup

validators.error_message: group_by must contain at least one field

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Inactivity timeout after which the story is closed with status 'timed_out'

fields.name: terminal_conditions
fields.description: Optional list of conditions that trigger immediate story closure

fields.name: chain_by
fields.description: Optional list of field paths for chain-by linking. On story close, these fields from the closing event register chain keys so subsequent events create a new linked story.

fields.name: chain_ttl
fields.description: TTL for chain registry entries. After expiry, chain keys are discarded.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model defining how events are grouped into stories. Specifies match predicates, group_by key extraction, timeout, optional terminal conditions, and optional chain_by linking.

types.name: Event

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Globally unique event identifier (UUID4)

fields.name: event_time
fields.description: Source-provided UTC timestamp of when the event occurred

fields.name: received_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of when the engine received the event

fields.name: sequence
fields.description: Monotonically increasing integer assigned by the engine for deterministic ordering

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: Identifier of the source that produced this event (e.g. 'webhook', 'otlp', 'sentinel')

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Opaque event data as a JSON-serializable dictionary

types.description: Immutable event envelope. Frozen Pydantic model carrying source metadata and an opaque payload dict. Events are the input to the correlation engine.

types.name: StoryStatus
types.description: Terminal status of a closed story.

types.name: Story

fields.name: story_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this story

fields.name: rule_name
fields.description: Name of the correlation rule that created this story

fields.name: group_key
fields.description: The composite key dict (field_name -> value) used for correlation lookup

fields.name: status
fields.description: Terminal status indicating how the story was closed

fields.name: events
fields.description: Ordered list of events in this story, sorted by (event_time, received_at, sequence)

fields.name: parent_story_id
fields.description: Story ID of the parent story if this story was created via chain_by linking. Empty string if no parent.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of when the story was first created

fields.name: closed_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of when the story was closed

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional metadata dict for extensibility

fields.name: event_count
fields.description: Number of events in the story (denormalized for convenience)

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model representing a completed story. Produced by _OpenStory.to_story() at close time. This is the public output type emitted to sinks.

types.name: CompositeKey

fields.name: rule_name
fields.description: Name of the correlation rule

fields.name: key_tuple
fields.description: Sorted list of [field_name, value] pairs extracted from event payload via group_by fields

types.description: The composite lookup key used in the correlation dict. Composed of rule_name and a sorted tuple of (field, value) pairs extracted from event payload via group_by.

types.name: ChainEntry

fields.name: parent_story_id
fields.description: ID of the story that closed and registered this chain entry

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Monotonic clock time at which this chain entry expires and should be discarded

types.description: Entry in the chain-by registry. Records the parent story ID and expiration time so subsequent events matching chain keys create linked stories.

types.name: RecordType
types.description: Discriminator for JSONL state records.

types.name: StoryOpenedRecord

fields.name: record_type
fields.description: Always 'story_opened'

fields.name: story_id

fields.name: rule_name

fields.name: group_key
fields.description: Composite key dict for this story

fields.name: parent_story_id
fields.description: Parent story ID if chain-linked, empty string otherwise

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of story creation

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Timeout in seconds from the correlation rule

types.description: JSONL state record emitted when a new story is created. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: EventAppendedRecord

fields.name: record_type
fields.description: Always 'event_appended'

fields.name: story_id
fields.description: ID of the story the event was appended to

fields.name: event
fields.description: The full event envelope that was appended

types.name: StoryClosedRecord

fields.name: record_type
fields.description: Always 'story_closed'

fields.name: story_id
fields.description: ID of the closed story

fields.name: status
fields.description: Terminal status of the story

fields.name: closed_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of when the story was closed

types.description: JSONL state record emitted when a story is closed (completed, timed_out, or evicted).

types.name: StateRecord
types.description: Discriminated union of all JSONL state record types, discriminated by record_type field.

types.name: SinkCallback
types.description: An async callable that accepts a closed Story and returns None. Signature: async (Story) -> None. Registered with StoryManager to receive closed stories.

types.name: ClockCallable
types.description: A callable returning monotonic time as float. Signature: () -> float. Defaults to time.monotonic. Injectable for testability.

types.name: IdFactory
types.description: A callable returning a new unique ID string. Signature: () -> str. Defaults to lambda: str(uuid4()). Injectable for deterministic test IDs.

types.name: FilePath

fields.name: value
fields.description: Absolute or relative file path

types.description: Filesystem path for JSONL state file.

types.name: RecoveryResult

fields.name: open_stories

fields.name: records_read
fields.description: Total number of valid records successfully read

fields.name: records_skipped
fields.description: Number of corrupt or malformed records skipped

fields.name: chain_entries

types.description: Result of replaying JSONL state file to recover open stories.

functions.name: CorrelationEngine.__init__
functions.description: Initialize the correlation engine with configuration, story manager reference, injectable clock, and injectable ID factory. Validates that all rule names are unique. Does NOT start the sweep task — that is done via start().

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Engine configuration including rules, limits, and state file path

inputs.name: story_manager
inputs.description: Reference to the StoryManager that owns open story lifecycle

inputs.name: clock
inputs.description: Monotonic clock callable, defaults to time.monotonic

inputs.name: id_factory
inputs.description: ID generator callable, defaults to uuid4

error_cases.name: duplicate_rule_name
error_cases.condition: Two or more rules share the same name

error_data.detail: Duplicate rule names found: {names}

error_cases.name: empty_rules
error_cases.condition: config.rules is empty

error_data.detail: At least one correlation rule is required

preconditions: All rule names in config.rules are unique
story_manager is initialized but not yet started
postconditions: Internal correlation dict is empty
Chain registry is empty
Monotonic sequence counter is 0
Rules are indexed for O(1) lookup by name

functions.name: CorrelationEngine.process_event
functions.description: Match an incoming event against all correlation rules. For each matching rule, extract the composite key via group_by fields, perform O(1) dict lookup for an existing open story. If found, append the event and check terminal conditions. If not found, check chain registry for a chain link and create a new story (with parent_story_id if chain-linked). Assigns sequence number and received_at timestamp. Wraps entire body in try/except — logs and drops events that cause errors, never crashes.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The incoming event to correlate

error_cases.name: no_matching_rule
error_cases.condition: Event does not match any correlation rule's match predicates

error_data.detail: Event dropped — no matching rule. Logged at DEBUG level.

error_cases.name: missing_group_by_field
error_cases.condition: Event payload missing a field specified in rule.group_by

error_data.detail: Event dropped — missing group_by field '{field}'. Logged at WARNING level.

error_cases.name: correlation_error
error_cases.condition: Any unexpected exception during processing

error_data.detail: Exception caught, logged at ERROR level, event dropped. Engine continues.

preconditions: Engine has been initialized with valid rules
Event has a valid event_id and payload
postconditions: Sequence counter incremented by 1
Event appended to matching open story OR new story created OR event dropped with log
If terminal condition matched, story closed via StoryManager
If story closed with chain_by, chain entry registered
State records written for any story creation or event append
side_effects: Writes state records to JSONL file
May trigger sink callbacks if story closes
Mutates internal correlation dict and chain registry

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts or updates entries in the O(1) correlation lookup dict
Registers chain entries on story close if rule has chain_by
Increments monotonic sequence counter
Appends StoryOpened and/or EventAppended records to JSONL state file
Logs dropped events, errors, and story lifecycle at appropriate levels

performance_budget.complexity: O(R) where R is the number of correlation rules; dict lookup is O(1) per rule

functions.name: CorrelationEngine.register_chain_entry
functions.description: Register a chain-by entry in the chain registry when a story with chain_by fields closes. Extracts chain key from the last event in the story and stores it with the parent story ID and expiration time.

inputs.name: story
inputs.description: The closed story whose chain_by fields should be registered

inputs.name: rule
inputs.description: The correlation rule that governed the closed story

error_cases.name: missing_chain_field
error_cases.condition: Last event in story missing a chain_by field

error_data.detail: Chain entry not registered — missing field '{field}' in last event. Logged at WARNING.

error_cases.name: empty_events
error_cases.condition: Story has no events (should not happen)

error_data.detail: Chain entry not registered — story has no events. Logged at ERROR.

postconditions: Chain registry contains new entry keyed by (rule_name, chain_key_tuple)
Entry expires_at is set to clock() + rule.chain_ttl

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts or overwrites a chain entry in the internal registry dict

functions.name: CorrelationEngine.purge_expired_chains
functions.description: Remove all expired entries from the chain registry. Called periodically by the sweep task or on demand.
postconditions: All chain entries with expires_at < clock() are removed
Return value is the count of purged entries

structured_side_effects.description: Removes expired entries from chain registry dict

performance_budget.complexity: O(C) where C is the number of chain registry entries

functions.name: CorrelationEngine.get_stats
functions.description: Return current engine statistics for monitoring and observability. Pure read, no side effects.
postconditions: Returned stats reflect current engine state at time of call

functions.name: StoryManager.__init__
functions.description: Initialize the story manager with max_open_stories limit, sweep interval, injectable clock, and empty OrderedDict for open stories. Does NOT start the sweep task.

inputs.name: max_open_stories
inputs.description: Upper bound on simultaneously open stories

inputs.name: sweep_interval
inputs.description: Interval in seconds between timeout sweep passes

inputs.name: clock
inputs.description: Monotonic clock callable, defaults to time.monotonic

postconditions: Internal OrderedDict is empty
Sink callback list is empty
Sweep task is not running

functions.name: StoryManager.register_sink
functions.description: Register an async callback to be invoked with each closed Story. Callbacks are invoked concurrently via asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True.

inputs.name: callback
inputs.description: Async callable accepting a Story and returning None

preconditions: callback is a valid async callable
postconditions: callback is appended to the internal sink callback list

structured_side_effects.description: Appends callback to the list of registered sink callbacks

functions.name: StoryManager.register_story
functions.description: Register a new open story. Adds to OrderedDict (most recently used position). If adding would exceed max_open_stories, evicts the least recently used story first (emitting it to sinks with status='evicted').

inputs.name: story_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the new story

inputs.name: rule_name
inputs.description: Correlation rule name that created this story

inputs.name: group_key
inputs.description: Composite key dict for this story

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: Inactivity timeout in seconds

inputs.name: parent_story_id
inputs.description: Parent story ID for chain-linked stories, empty string if none

inputs.name: created_at
inputs.description: UTC timestamp of story creation

error_cases.name: duplicate_story_id
error_cases.condition: story_id already exists in open stories

error_data.detail: Story {story_id} already registered

error_cases.name: eviction_sink_error
error_cases.condition: A sink callback raises during eviction emission

error_data.detail: Sink error during eviction logged at ERROR level, processing continues

preconditions: story_id is not already present in the open story dict
postconditions: Story is present in the OrderedDict at the most-recently-used position
If a story was evicted, it has been emitted to all sinks with status='evicted'
len(open_stories) <= max_open_stories

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts new story into OrderedDict, may evict LRU story
Logs eviction events at WARNING level

functions.name: StoryManager.append_event
functions.description: Append an event to an existing open story and move it to most-recently-used position in the OrderedDict.

inputs.name: story_id
inputs.description: ID of the story to append to

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: Event to append

error_cases.name: story_not_found
error_cases.condition: story_id not in open stories

error_data.detail: Story {story_id} not found in open stories

preconditions: story_id exists in the open story dict
postconditions: Event is appended to the story's event list
Story is at most-recently-used position in OrderedDict
Story's last_activity_time is updated to clock()

structured_side_effects.description: Appends event to story, updates LRU position and activity timestamp

functions.name: StoryManager.touch_story
functions.description: Move a story to the most-recently-used position in the OrderedDict without appending an event. Updates last_activity_time.

inputs.name: story_id
inputs.description: ID of the story to touch

error_cases.name: story_not_found
error_cases.condition: story_id not in open stories

error_data.detail: Story {story_id} not found in open stories

preconditions: story_id exists in the open story dict
postconditions: Story is at the most-recently-used position
Story's last_activity_time is updated to clock()

structured_side_effects.description: Moves story to end of OrderedDict, updates activity timestamp

functions.name: StoryManager.close_story
functions.description: Close an open story with the given status. Removes from OrderedDict, converts internal _OpenStory to frozen Story via to_story(), emits to all registered sink callbacks via asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True. Sink errors are logged but do not propagate.

inputs.name: story_id
inputs.description: ID of the story to close

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Terminal status for the story

error_cases.name: story_not_found
error_cases.condition: story_id not in open stories

error_data.detail: Story {story_id} not found in open stories

error_cases.name: sink_callback_error
error_cases.condition: One or more sink callbacks raise an exception

error_data.detail: Sink error logged at ERROR level. Story still closed and returned.

preconditions: story_id exists in the open story dict
postconditions: Story is removed from the OrderedDict
Returned Story is a frozen Pydantic model with events sorted by (event_time, received_at, sequence)
All registered sink callbacks have been invoked (errors logged, not propagated)
Story.status matches the provided status
Story.closed_at is set to current UTC time

structured_side_effects.description: Removes story from OrderedDict
Invokes all registered async sink callbacks with the frozen Story
Logs story closure and any sink errors

functions.name: StoryManager.start_sweep_task
functions.description: Start the periodic async background task that sweeps for timed-out stories. Uses injectable clock for testability. The task runs in a loop with asyncio.sleep(sweep_interval), checking each open story's last_activity_time against its timeout.

error_cases.name: already_running
error_cases.condition: Sweep task is already active

error_data.detail: Sweep task is already running

preconditions: Sweep task is not already running
postconditions: An asyncio.Task is created and stored internally
The task will periodically close stories whose inactivity exceeds their timeout

structured_side_effects.description: Creates and stores an asyncio.Task for periodic timeout sweeping

functions.name: StoryManager.stop_sweep_task
functions.description: Cancel the periodic sweep task and await its completion. Safe to call if not running (no-op).
postconditions: Sweep task is cancelled and awaited
Internal task reference is cleared

structured_side_effects.description: Cancels and clears the async sweep task

functions.name: StoryManager.sweep_once
functions.description: Perform a single timeout sweep pass. Iterates open stories, closes any whose (clock() - last_activity_time) exceeds their timeout with status='timed_out'. Returns the number of stories closed. Exposed publicly for testing.

error_cases.name: close_error
error_cases.condition: Error during close_story for a timed-out story

error_data.detail: Error logged at ERROR level, sweep continues to next story

postconditions: All stories exceeding their timeout have been closed with status='timed_out'
Return value is the count of stories closed in this sweep

structured_side_effects.description: Removes timed-out stories from OrderedDict
Emits closed stories to sink callbacks
Logs timeout closures

performance_budget.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of open stories

functions.name: StoryManager.get_open_story_count
functions.description: Return the current number of open stories. Pure read.
postconditions: Return value equals len(open_stories)

functions.name: StoryManager.load_recovered_stories
functions.description: Load recovered stories from state recovery into the OrderedDict. Used during startup after recover_state. Stories are inserted in creation order. If count exceeds max_open_stories, oldest are evicted.

inputs.name: recovery_result
inputs.description: Result from recover_state containing open stories and chain entries to restore

error_cases.name: invalid_recovery_data
error_cases.condition: A recovered story dict is malformed or missing required fields

error_data.detail: Malformed story skipped with WARNING log, recovery continues

preconditions: StoryManager OrderedDict is empty (fresh initialization or after shutdown)
postconditions: All non-evicted recovered stories are present in OrderedDict
Return value is the number of stories loaded
len(open_stories) <= max_open_stories

structured_side_effects.description: Populates OrderedDict with recovered stories
Logs recovery progress and any skipped stories

functions.name: append_record
functions.description: Append a single state record to the JSONL state file. Uses aiofiles for async I/O. Each record is serialized via Pydantic model_dump(mode='json') and written as a single line followed by newline. Flushes after write. I/O errors are caught and logged — never crashes the engine.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Path to the JSONL state file

inputs.name: record
inputs.description: The state record to append

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: File write or flush fails (disk full, permissions, etc.)

error_data.detail: I/O error logged at ERROR level with record context. Engine continues.

error_cases.name: serialization_error
error_cases.condition: Record fails to serialize to JSON

error_data.detail: Serialization error logged at ERROR level. Record dropped.

preconditions: path is writable or the parent directory exists and is writable
postconditions: Record is appended as a single JSON line to the file
File is flushed after write
On I/O error: error is logged, no exception propagates

structured_side_effects.description: Appends one JSON line to the JSONL state file
Logs I/O and serialization errors

functions.name: recover_state
functions.description: Read the JSONL state file and replay all records to reconstruct the set of open stories at the time of last shutdown. Skips corrupt/malformed lines with warning logs. Returns a RecoveryResult with open stories (those with StoryOpened but no corresponding StoryClosed) and recovered chain entries.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Path to the JSONL state file

inputs.name: clock
inputs.description: Monotonic clock for computing chain entry expiry relative to recovery time

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: State file does not exist

error_data.detail: Returns empty RecoveryResult. Logged at INFO level.

error_cases.name: corrupt_line
error_cases.condition: A line in the JSONL file is not valid JSON or fails deserialization

error_data.detail: Line skipped with WARNING log including line number. Recovery continues.

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: File read fails due to permissions or other I/O error

error_data.detail: Unrecoverable I/O error during state recovery

postconditions: Returned open_stories contains only stories with StoryOpened record but no corresponding StoryClosed record
Each open story has its full event list from replayed EventAppended records
records_skipped counts all malformed lines
records_read + records_skipped equals total lines in file
If file does not exist, returns empty RecoveryResult with all counts 0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads entire JSONL state file line by line for replay
Logs recovery progress, corrupt lines, and results

performance_budget.complexity: O(L) where L is the number of lines in the state file

invariants: INV-01: The number of open stories in StoryManager never exceeds max_open_stories
INV-02: The correlation dict key (rule_name, key_tuple) maps to at most one open story at any time
INV-03: Every event in a closed Story is sorted by (event_time, received_at, sequence) ascending
INV-04: The monotonic sequence counter is strictly increasing across all processed events
INV-05: Evicted stories are always emitted to sinks with status='evicted' before removal
INV-06: Chain registry entries are never used after their expires_at time
INV-07: A story created via chain_by always has a non-empty parent_story_id
INV-08: Stories cannot be modified after close_story returns — the returned Story is frozen (Pydantic frozen=True)
INV-09: State file records are strictly append-only — no in-place modifications or truncation during normal operation
INV-10: Sink callback errors never prevent story closure or crash the engine
INV-11: The OrderedDict in StoryManager maintains LRU order — least recently used story is always at position 0
INV-12: recover_state produces the same set of open stories regardless of how many times it is called on the same file (idempotent)
INV-13: Events missing required group_by fields are logged and dropped — they never create or modify stories

data_access.rationale: Reads incoming Event payloads which may contain PII fields depending on source (e.g. Sentinel incidents with user identifiers, webhook payloads with IP addresses). Writes JSONL state records containing full event envelopes and story metadata to disk. Writes closed Story objects to sink callbacks which may propagate to external systems. All event payload data is passed through unmodified — this component does not filter PII.

side_effects.rationale: Appends StoryOpened, EventAppended, and StoryClosed records to JSONL state file via aiofiles. Records contain full event references and story metadata which may include PII from source payloads.
Invokes registered async sink callbacks with frozen Story objects on close/eviction. Story contains accumulated events whose payloads may include PII. Sinks are fire-and-forget with error logging.
Reads JSONL state file on startup for recovery, deserializing all stored state records to reconstruct open stories. File contains historical event data that may include PII.

authority.rationale: This component is the sole owner of story lifecycle (creation, event accumulation, closing, eviction), correlation rule evaluation and chain-by registry, and the JSONL state records that persist story state. No other component may create, modify, or close stories.
